title: Music
content:
When music sounds, gone is the earth I know,
     And all her lovely things even lovelier grow;
     Her flowers in vision flame, her forest trees
     Lift burdened branches, stilled with ecstasies.

     When music sounds, out of the water rise
     Naiads whose beauty dims my waking eyes,
     Rapt in strange dreams burns each enchanted face,
     With solemn echoing stirs their dwelling-place.

     When music sounds, all that I was I am
     Ere to this haunt of brooding dust I came;
     While from Time's woods break into distant song
     The swift-winged hours, as I haste along.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
